ddls:
CREATE TABLE "experiments" (
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each experiment */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the research project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the experiment started */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the experiment ended */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the experiment's purpose and methodology */,
  "sampling_method" TEXT /* Method used for selecting subjects */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response expected (e.g., binary, continuous) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the experiment (e.g., planned, ongoing, completed) */,
  "ethics_approval" INTEGER /* Whether the experiment has ethics approval */,
  "principal_investigator" TEXT /* Name or ID of the principal investigator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Full name of the subject */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the subject at the time of the experiment */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the subject */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the subject (if applicable) */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the subject */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the subject */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education completed */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Current occupation */,
  "consent_form" INTEGER /* Whether the subject has signed a consent form */,
  "consent_date" TEXT /* Date the consent form was signed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the experiment associated with the response */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* ID of the subject providing the response */,
  "sample" INTEGER /* Sample index for the response */,
  "rt_sampled" REAL /* Reaction time sampled during the response */,
  "response_sampled" INTEGER /* Indicator if the response is recorded */,
  "rt" REAL /* Calculated reaction time */,
  "response" INTEGER /* Binary response value indicating success/failure or yes/no */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Condition under which the response was recorded (e.g., control, treatment) */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session during which the response was recorded */,
  "device_used" TEXT /* Device used to record the response */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment in which the response was recorded (e.g., lab, home) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or observations about the response */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_responses_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for account security */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "experiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the experiment being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action taken (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Whether the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_experiment_id FOREIGN KEY ("experiment_id") REFERENCES experiments ("experiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_accounts ("user_id")
)
